commit.message:
Fix ticker schedule accuracy and add day-duration overflow guard (#4)

Two correctness issues in the recurring sync interval logic: the "next
run" timestamp was anchored to the wrong point in time, and day-based
durations could silently overflow `int64`, causing a panic in
`time.NewTicker`.

## Changes

**`cmd/collection-sync/main.go`**
- Move `time.NewTicker` to before the initial sync so the schedule is
anchored to process start, not sync completion — the first "next run"
message was previously off by the sync duration
- Replace `time.Now().Add(interval)` inside the loop with
`tick.Add(interval)` — `tick` is the ticker's scheduled fire time, so
this correctly reflects the next tick even when sync duration exceeds
the interval

**`internal/config/duration.go`**
- Guard against `time.Duration` overflow when converting large day
values; return an explicit error instead of producing a negative
duration that panics the ticker

```go
maxDays := int64(time.Duration(1<<63-1) / (24 * time.Hour))
if int64(days) > maxDays {
    return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid interval %q: exceeds maximum supported duration", s)
}
```

---------

Add TARGET_APPLE to System.Private.CoreLib DefineConstants for Apple platforms (#126652)

`TypeLoadException: Could not load type
'System.Runtime.InteropServices.Swift.SwiftSelf' from assembly
'System.Private.CoreLib'` on tvos_arm64 CoreCLR. The Swift interop types
(`SwiftSelf`, `SwiftError`, `SwiftIndirectResult`) were being trimmed
from `System.Private.CoreLib.dll` by ILLink on Apple builds.

The `CreateRuntimeRootILLinkDescriptorFile` task generates the ILLink
root descriptor by processing `corelib.h` with
`DefineConstants="$(DefineConstants);FOR_ILLINK"`. Since `corelib.h`
gates the Swift type definitions behind `#ifdef TARGET_APPLE`, and
`TARGET_APPLE` was absent from the C# `DefineConstants`, the task didn't
emit preservation roots for these types. ILLink then trimmed them as
unreachable, causing `CoreLibBinder::GetClass(CLASS__SWIFT_SELF)` to
throw at runtime.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/126557

---------

Co-authored-by: jkotas <6668460+jkotas@users.noreply.github.com>

Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

Fix global.json SDK version, SharpMUSH.Generated.generated.sln project reference, and MemgraphDatabase async enumerable race condition (#543)

* Fix global.json SDK version from 10.0.0 to 10.0.100

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/2a61f9d0-5a18-4abd-94f1-490eda5e34f2

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/7c0166d6-0167-4ddb-b3c4-a81a17b82acc

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore race in MemgraphDatabase async enumerables

Add FreshEnumerable<T> wrapper that calls the factory on every GetAsyncEnumerator()
call, ensuring each enumeration gets a brand-new async state machine instead of
cloning the original cached one.

Previously, async IAsyncEnumerable<T> state machines were created once and cached
inside Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<T>>. The C# runtime's GetAsyncEnumerator() clone
mechanism reuses internal fields (including ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore) in ways
that produce a race when the same IAsyncEnumerable is enumerated concurrently -
particularly via the .GetAwaiter().GetResult() path in SharpMUSHBooleanExpressionVisitor's
lock-expression trees, which blocks thread pool threads and amplifies contention.

Fixes all Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> and AsyncLazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> factories in
MapNodeToSharpObject, MapToSharpAttribute, and MapToLazySharpAttribute.

commit.message:
Two-period spending money with 3-day bill lead time

Overhaul useSpendingMoney to look across two pay periods:
- Period 1: checking balance minus bills due before next paycheck
- + Paycheck incoming
- Period 2: minus bills due before second paycheck
- Budget items prorated across the full window
- = Free Spending

Bills use a 3-day lead time for period slotting — a bill due on
the 1st is effectively slotted into the EOM pay period (due date
minus 3 days = 28th/29th). This ensures the mortgage and similar
bills are attributed to the paycheck that actually needs to cover
them, not the one after the due date.

Update SpendingMoneyBreakdown to show both periods separately
with individual bill line items, the incoming paycheck, and the
budget waterfall.

used default serialization (enums as integers). When the frontend loaded

silently skipped, producing an empty chat window.

update all frontend enum constants from integers to matching strings.

fix: pre-fetch UTXO address before write lock to prevent spawn_blocking stall

Both the anchor check and old_addr_to_remove squatter-eviction check were calling
utxo_manager.get_utxo().await (which uses spawn_blocking internally) while holding
masternodes.write().await.  This caused tasks to queue on the write lock and starved
tokio workers, producing the RPC-timeout / watchdog-restart cycle seen on live nodes.

Fix: look up the UTXO address once before taking the write lock (prefetched_utxo_addr),
then reuse that value inside the lock for both checks.

fix: skip wildcard atom type '00' during OpenMM XML export

MM3/Tinker force fields use '00' as a wildcard meaning 'any atom type'.
OpenMM XML has no wildcard concept, so exporting these terms would
create silently non-functional class entries. Now skips them with a
warning log message.
